title: Tailgating Detection
dataSources: Stream normalized door and credential events from the **Access control system** into `index=physical` as `sourcetype="access_control"` with extracted fields **`badge_id`**, **`badge_holder`**, **`door`**, and **`action`** (for example `entry` versus `exit` or `denied`). Typical collection uses Splunk Connect for Syslog ([Splunkbase app 4740](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4740)), a Heavy Forwarder `inputs.conf` syslog listener, or HTTPS posts to Splunk HEC `/services/collector/event` when the head-end exports JSON; align controller clocks to sub-second skew so `transaction maxspan` windows reflect true reader sequencing.
spl:
index=physical sourcetype="access_control" door="DC_Main_Entry"
| transaction badge_id maxspan=10s
| where eventcount > 1 AND action="entry"
| table _time, badge_holder, badge_id, eventcount
description: Flags likely tailgating at supervised data-center doors when the Access control system records more than one `action=entry` for the same `badge_id` within a short windowâ€”often before an anti-passback exit clears the zoneâ€”surfacing piggyback entry that a single credential alone would not justify.
value: Undetected tailgating erodes one-person-one-badge guarantees for data halls and mantraps, weakens forensic reconstruction after insider or visitor incidents, and undermines assurance narratives for customers and auditors who expect physical entry to match accountable identities.
implementation: Confirm anti-passback and tailgate policies are enforced on high-assurance readers, normalize `action` and `badge_id` values at ingest, forward events with stable timestamps, then run the catalog transaction search on `index=physical` targeting production mantrap and data-hall doors. Tune `maxspan`, suppress known escort or maintenance workflows with lookup tags, alert on sustained violation counts per `door`, and route notifications to physical security operations with drill-based runbook steps.
visualization: Sortable Table of `_time`, `badge_holder`, `badge_id`, `eventcount`, and `door`; column Bar chart of tailgating-like bursts by `door`; timechart of weekly incident counts; Single value KPI for distinct `badge_id` affected in the period; optional drilldown link to raw events for CCTV correlation.
cimSpl:
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication
  by Authentication.user, Authentication.action, Authentication.src span=1h
| sort - count
dataModelAcceleration: Keep primary detection on raw `index=physical` `sourcetype="access_control"` because `transaction` is not tstats-friendly; only enable Authentication data-model acceleration after FIELDALIAS maps `badge_holder` to user and volume review confirms sparse tailgating slices will not waste build slots.

references.title: Splunk Documentation â€” Create and manage alerts
Splunk Documentation â€” Forward data to Splunk Enterprise

knownFalsePositives: (1) **Escorted visitor batches at `DC_Main_Entry`** â€” Security staff may badge once and hold the door during tours, producing multiple `entry` reads on one `badge_id`; suppress when `visit_id`, `escort_flag`, or guard shift tags are present in the feed, or throttle during scheduled visitor windows. (2) **Reader bounce / double-tap at the same leaf** â€” Mechanical chatter or users tapping twice can inflate `eventcount` inside `maxspan=10s`; differentiate using distinct `reader_id` if exported, or raise `maxspan` slightly after comparing controller raw traces. (3) **Controller syslog replay after HA failover** â€” Standby nodes may resend historical rows and duplicate `entry` tuples; require active-primary markers (`controller_role`) or dedupe on `_cd` before paging. (4) **Maintenance card test mode** â€” Technicians cycling `entry`/`exit` during commissioning triggers stacked `entry` actions; mute when `maintenance_mode=true` on the door or when ServiceNow change tags cover the interval.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
â€˘ Ingest path approved for the **Access control system**: Splunk Connect for Syslog ([Splunkbase 4740](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4740)) or `inputs.conf` on a Heavy Forwarder receiving syslog/TLS from the PACS head-end, with optional HEC `POST /services/collector/event` for JSON bridges.
â€˘ Events land on `index=physical` with `sourcetype="access_control"` and populated `badge_id`, `badge_holder`, `door`, and `action` fieldsâ€”verify in Search using a `-15m` window before promoting the alert.
â€˘ RBAC: grant a dedicated role such as `physical_security_analyst` search rights on `index=physical` only; schedule the detection under a service account with `schedule_search` and `edit_alert_actions` while keeping secrets out of dashboards.
â€˘ Clock sync: require NTP-stable controllers so `transaction` groups reflect real reader order; document maintenance windows when controllers restart.

Step 1 â€” Configure data collection
Map door IDs (for example `DC_Main_Entry` or internal mantrap leaves) consistently between the controller and Splunk; ensure `action` distinguishes entry, exit, forced, and denied outcomes the transaction logic can interpret.
â€˘ If multiple sites share one index, add `site` or `campus` at ingest via `props.conf` transforms for later filtering.

Step 2 â€” Create the search and alert
Save the catalog SPL as a report, clone to a scheduled alert with `cron_schedule=*/5 * * * *`, `dispatch.earliest_time=-5m`, `dispatch.latest_time=now`, and `alert.suppress` keyed on `door` plus `badge_id` for at least two intervals to limit duplicate pages.

```spl
index=physical sourcetype="access_control" door="DC_Main_Entry"
| transaction badge_id maxspan=10s
| where eventcount > 1 AND action="entry"
| table _time, badge_holder, badge_id, eventcount
```

Understanding this SPL
â€˘ The base filter uses `index=physical`, `sourcetype="access_control"`, and the example `door="DC_Main_Entry"`â€”clone additional alerts per critical door or replace with a tokenized lookup of high-risk portals.
â€˘ `transaction badge_id maxspan=10s` collapses rapid sequences from the same credential; tighten or widen `maxspan` if reader debounce or vestibule geometry differs.
â€˘ `where eventcount > 1 AND action="entry"` highlights multiple entry-stamped events in one burst, the tailgating heuristic for this UC; pair with controller anti-passback alarms when available.
â€˘ `table` projects analyst-friendly columns for ticketing and CCTV correlation.

Step 3 â€” Validate
Execute a supervised tailgating drill on a non-production reader, export the controllerâ€™s event log for the same minute, and confirm Splunk returns the expected `eventcount` and `badge_holder` values; repeat after an intentionally benign single-badge walk-through to confirm silence.

Step 4 â€” Operationalize and troubleshoot
Publish a dashboard with the results table, a timechart of incident counts by `door`, and drilldown to raw events; if noise spikes, review syslog queue depth on the forwarder, HEC ACK failures, or replay bursts after controller failover documented in your physical-security runbook.
grandmaExplanation: We watch the physical data center for power, cooling, environment, and physical access.

controlTest.positiveScenario: During a controlled drill at `DC_Main_Entry`, two rapid `action=entry` events share the same `badge_id` within ten seconds; the saved search using `transaction badge_id maxspan=10s` returns a row with `eventcount>1` matching the controllerâ€™s live tail, and the physical security analyst records the Splunk job SID alongside the head-end alarm printout.
controlTest.negativeScenario: For a thirty-minute window, operators ingest only isolated single-tap `entry` events followed by matching `exit` rows for each `badge_id` with no overlapping entries; the detection returns zero rows after the `where eventcount > 1` stage even though `index=physical` volume is normal, proving benign traffic does not page.

evidence: Archive the alert or saved-report output showing `_time`, `badge_holder`, `badge_id`, and `eventcount` for the firing interval, retain the Splunk job identifier, and store the matching Access control system event export or controller screenshot for the same door and timestamp range.
exclusions: Does not replace video analytics-only tailgating models, certify door hardware interlocks, or prove logical identity lifecycle; credential provisioning, visitor badging policy, and non-Splunk forensic tools are out of scope unless their signals are already normalized into `access_control` events.
equipmentModels: Physical access control head-end exporting syslog or HEC to Splunk
